facts.statement: American Express Company is a global payments and premium lifestyle brand powered by technology.
The company was founded in 1850 and is headquartered in New York.
Its card-issuing, merchant-acquiring and card network businesses offer products and services to consumers, small businesses, mid-sized companies and large corporations around the world.
Management says the company seeks to grow by focusing on five strategic imperatives, and in January 2026 it added a fifth imperative focused on reimagining customer and colleague experiences.
SEC company facts show FY2025 revenue of $37.218 billion and FY2025 net income of $8.374 billion.
SEC company facts show FY2025 operating cash flow of $18.559 billion.
SEC company facts show FY2025 stockholders equity of $24.711 billion and long-term debt of $49.715 billion.
The 2026 proxy statement says total revenues net of interest expense reached an all-time high of $72.2 billion in 2025, up 10% reported and 9% FX-adjusted versus the prior year.
The 2026 proxy statement reports diluted EPS of $15.38 for 2025, up 10% versus the prior year, or 15% excluding the prior-year gain on sale of Accertify.
The company reported a 2025 return on equity of 34%.
The company says it returned $7.6 billion of capital to shareholders in 2025 through share repurchases and dividends.
The 2026 proxy statement reports $5.3 billion of share repurchases in 2025.
The 2026 proxy statement reports $2.3 billion of dividends in 2025.
The company says it added 12.5 million new proprietary cards in 2025 and ended the year with 152.8 million cards-in-force issued on its global network.

The company says it reached over 170 million American Express-accepting merchant locations worldwide, with its locations-in-force estimate reflecting about 37 million merchants in China including digital-wallet acceptance.
More than 70% of the new accounts acquired in 2025 were on fee-paying products.
The company says total billed business grew 8% reported, or 7% FX-adjusted, in 2025, with growth across geographies and across Goods & Services and Travel & Entertainment categories.

The company says net write-off and delinquency rates remained best-in-class in 2025, and its 10-K tables show a reserve for credit losses of $6.412 billion and non-accrual loans of $664 million at December 31, 2025.
The 10-K states that reserve for credit losses at December 31, 2025 was allocated 92% to Card Member loans, 3% to Card Member receivables and 5% to Other loans.

The 2026 proxy statement reports total loans and card member receivables of $224.8 billion in 2025; the 10-K table shows total loans of $162.760 billion and total card member receivables of $62.031 billion at December 31, 2025.
The 10-K says total deposits were $152.5 billion as of December 31, 2025, of which approximately $13.0 billion were uninsured.
The 2026 proxy statement identifies Stephen J. Squeri as Chairman, Chief Executive Officer and Director, and Christophe Y. Le Caillec as Chief Financial Officer.
The 2026 proxy statement lists 13 director nominees for election to one-year terms.

The 2026 proxy statement says the executive compensation program uses variable pay over annual and multi-year periods, with long-term incentive awards designed to align executives with long-term performance and a company scorecard covering Shareholder, Customer, Colleague and Strategic categories.
The 2026 proxy statement says the board oversees risk management, including separate sections on cybersecurity risk oversight and board oversight of AI.
The 2026 proxy statement states that the company has no cumulative voting rights and that abstentions and broker non-votes are not considered votes cast on the proposals described.
